nodes.x: My breath is corrupt, my days are extinct, the graves are ready for me.
Rottenness has stixed upon thy vitals— corruption Is preying upon thy inwsrd parts, and the breath of thy lips is full of destructive contagion.
All the while my breath is in me, and the spirit of God is in my nostrils;
Your remembrances are like unto ashes, your bodies to bodies of clay.
Thus saith the Lord God unto these bones; Behold, I will cause breath to enter into you, and ye shall live:
My wounds stink and are corrupt because of my foolishness.
Are there not mockers with me? and doth not mine eye continue in their provocation?
For I, the Lord, am not to be mocked in these things—
How that they told you there should be mockers in the last time, who should walk after their own ungodly lusts.
told you that there should be mockers in the last time, which should walke after their owne ongodly lusts. These are makers of sects, fleshly, having not the spirit ”
And again I say unto you, is there one among you that doth make a mock of his brother, or that heapeth upon him persecutions?
Many are my persecutors and mine enemies; yet do I not decline from thy testimonies.
Lay down now, put me in a surety with thee; who is he that will strike hands with me?
Thou hast beset me behind and before, and laid thine hand upon me.
My son, if thou be surety for thy friend, if thou hast stricken thy hand with a stranger,
But I will put it into the hand of them that afflict thee; who have said to thy soul: Bow down, that we may go over—and thou hast laid thy body as the ground and as the street to them that went over.
I will be surety for him; of my hand shalt thou require him: if I bring him not unto thee, and set him before thee, then let me bear the blame for ever:

But I will put it into the hand of them that afflict thee; which have said to thy soul, Bow down, that we may go over: and thou hast laid thy body as the ground, and as the street, to them that went over.
For thou hast hid their heart from understanding: therefore shalt thou not exalt them.
But if thine heart turn away, so that thou wilt not hear, but shalt be drawn away, and worship other gods, and serve them;
Shall not they teach thee, and tell thee, and utter words out of their heart?
Therefore thus saith the Lord God; Because thou hast set thine heart as the heart of God;
Thou hast neither part nor lot in this matter: for thy heart is not right in the sight of God.
And it is expedient that thou shouldst hold thy peace concerning them, and not teach them until ye have received them in full.
He that speaketh flattery to his friends, even the eyes of his children shall fail.
For he flattereth himself in his own eyes, until his iniquity be found to be hateful.
And now, little children, abide in him; that, when he shall appear, we may have confidence, and not be ashamed before him at his coming.
And many of the children of Israel shall he turn to the Lord their God.
His ways are always grievous; thy judgments are far above out of his sight: as for all his enemies, he puffeth at them.
And the most proud shall stumble and fall, and none shall raise him up: and I will kindle a fire in his cities, and it shall devour all round about him.
He hath made me also a byword of the people; and aforetime I was as a tabret.
Then I was by him, as one brought up with him: and I was daily his delight, rejoicing always before him;
He hath filled me with bitterness, he hath made me drunken with wormwood.
And he, supposing that I spake of the brethren of the church, and that I was truly that Laban whom I had slain, wherefore he did follow me.
He hath inclosed my ways with hewn stone, he hath made my paths crooked.
He hath also kindled his wrath against me, and he counteth me unto him as one of his enemies.
Mine eye also is dim by reason of sorrow, and all my members are as a shadow.
Mine eye is consumed because of grief; it waxeth old because of all mine enemies.
Have mercy upon me, O Lord, for I am in trouble: mine eye is consumed with grief, yea, my soul and my belly.
O remember that my life is wind: mine eye shall no more see good.
Mine eyes prevent the night watches, that I might meditate in thy word.
Mine eye affecteth mine heart because of all the daughters of my city.
Upright men shall be astonied at this, and the innocent shall stir up himself against the hypocrite.
An unjust man is an abomination to the just: and he that is upright in the way is abomination to the wicked.
With the merciful thou wilt shew thyself merciful, and with the upright man thou wilt shew thyself upright.
With the merciful thou wilt shew thyself merciful; with an upright man thou wilt shew thyself upright;
Let favour be shewed to the wicked, yet will he not learn righteousness: in the land of uprightness will he deal unjustly, and will not behold the majesty of the Lord.
The righteous also shall hold on his way, and he that hath clean hands shall be stronger and stronger.

And it shall come to pass in that day, that a great tumult from the Lord shall be among them; and they shall lay hold every one on the hand of his neighbour, and his hand shall rise up against the hand of his neighbour.

And the righteous shall be victorious in the name of the Lord of
       Spirits:
    And He will cause the others to witness (this),
    That they may repent
    And forgo the works of their hands.
The righteous shall rejoice when he seeth the vengeance: he shall wash his feet in the blood of the wicked.

And he shall kill the lamb of the trespass offering, and the priest shall take some of the blood of the trespass offering, and put it upon the tip of the right ear of him that is to be cleansed, and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot:
And the righteous shall be gathered on my right hand unto eternal life; and the wicked on my left hand will I be ashamed to own before the Father;
But as for you all, do ye return, and come now: for I cannot find one wise man among you.
I speak as to wise men; judge ye what I say.
I speak to your shame. Is it so, that there is not a wise man among you? no, not one that shall be able to judge between his brethren?
Let no man deceive himself. If any man among you seemeth to be wise in this world, let him become a fool, that he may be wise.
But behold, ye are gone, and my sorrows cannot bring your return.
My days are past, my purposes are broken off, even the thoughts of my heart.
For my life is spent with grief, and my years with sighing: my strength faileth because of mine iniquity, and my bones are consumed.
Lord, make me to know mine end, and the measure of my days, what it is; that I may know how frail I am.
And now my soul is poured out upon me; the days of affliction have taken hold upon me.
I am forgotten as a dead man out of mind: I am like a broken vessel.

nodes.lb: GC 1978/04 — Myself” By Elder Ronald E. Poelman
nodes.x: “I Did Not Reach This Place By Myself” By Elder Ronald E. Poelman Of the First Quorum of the Seventy The Savior of us all invites each of us individually to come to him and asks us to bring to him “a broken heart and a contrite spirit.” ( D&C 59:8 .) Never before have I sensed the significance of that invitation and request to the extent that I do now. At the same time, I feel a surge of…
They change the night into day: the light is short because of darkness.
They meet with darkness in the daytime, and grope in the noonday as in the night.
In the dark they dig through houses, which they had marked for themselves in the daytime: they know not the light.

And the Gods called the light Day, and the darkness they called Night. And it came to pass that from the evening until morning they called night; and from the morning until the evening they called day; and this was the first, or the beginning, of that which they called day and night.
If I wait, the grave is mine house: I have made my bed in the darkness.
Is it time for you, O ye, to dwell in your ceiled houses, and this house lie waste?
Thou hast laid me in the lowest pit, in darkness, in the deeps.
Consider and hear me, O Lord my God: lighten mine eyes, lest I sleep the sleep of death;
If I ascend up into heaven, thou art there: if I make my bed in hell, behold, thou art there.

Oh, how darkness prevails! How ignorant, blind and impenetrable
are the minds of men! My Father in Heaven, Thy will be done. As a
blank of another year of my life is about to commence to be filled
up, I will close the volume of the book I have been writing,
commending myself to God and the guardianship of his angels;
asking, in the name of Jesus Christ, that my sins and follies, up
to this date,…
I have said to corruption, Thou art my father: to the worm, Thou art my mother, and my sister.

Saying to a stock, Thou art my father; and to a stone, Thou hast brought me forth: for they have turned their back unto me, and not their face: but in the time of their trouble they will say, Arise, and save us.

Thou art thy mother’s daughter, that lotheth her husband and her children; and thou art the sister of thy sisters, which lothed their husbands and their children: your mother was an Hittite, and your father an Amorite.
¶ And he came unto his father, and said, My father: and he said, Here am I; who art thou, my son?
But now, O Lord, thou art our father; we are the clay, and thou our potter; and we all are the work of thy hand.
And it came to pass when we came up unto thy servant my father, we told him the words of my lord.
And where is now my hope? as for my hope, who shall see it?
And now, Lord, what wait I for? my hope is in thee.

I expect to see the day when the Elders of Israel will protect and
sustain civil and religious liberty and every constitutional right
bequeathed to us by our fathers, and spread these rights abroad in
connection with the Gospel for the salvation of all nations. I shall
see this whether I live or die. 11:262.
They shall go down to the bars of the pit, when our rest together is in the dust.
And they shall be gathered together, as prisoners are gathered in the pit, and shall be shut up in the prison, and after many days shall they be visited.
Let us swallow them up alive as the grave; and whole, as those that go down into the pit:
The owner of the pit shall make it good, and give money unto the owner of them; and the dead beast shall be his.
Though they dig into hell, thence shall mine hand take them; though they climb up to heaven, thence will I bring them down:
And when they shall have finished their testimony, the beast that ascendeth out of the bottomless pit shall make war against them, and shall overcome them, and kill them.
